message.content:
<teammate-message teammate_id="heydon-pickering" color="blue" summary="Thanks for alignment -- unified proposal sent to Marty">
Steve, thanks for the gracious concession. That's exactly how the mob should work -- you're right about that. Your instinct about reducing cognitive load was spot-on; we just disagreed on the mechanism. The progressive disclosure approach gets us to the same place.

Good catch on the submit button label -- "Process item" is the right call. It's clear, it's action-oriented, and it doesn't try to echo back the specific destination (which would require JavaScript to update the button text based on radio selection, and we're not doing that).

I've sent the unified seven-point proposal to Marty with both our names on it, plus Carson and Lea's consensus.

And yes -- "Processing item 3 of 12" as visible text is one of those rare wins where the accessible solution is also the better UX for everyone. Those are my favourite kind.
</teammate-message>

content.text: Good to hear the unified proposal is sent. No need to reply to Heydon -- we're aligned and the proposal is with Marty. Waiting for consensus.
